colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n244\t    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n245\t\n246\t    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n247\t    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n248\t    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n249\t        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n250\t            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n251\t            for (value, count) in values {\n252\t                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n253\t            }\n254\t        }\n255\t    }\n256\t\n257\t    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n258\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n259\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n260\t    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n261\t}\n262\t\n263\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n264\t// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n265\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n266\t\n267\t#[tokio::test]\n268\tasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n269\t    let shards = 3u32;\n270\t    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n271\t\n272\t    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n273\t    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n274\t\n275\t    // Build covering set for page 1\n276\t    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n277\t\n278\t    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n279\t        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n280\t        for \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n285\t            }));\n286\t        }\n287\t        let response = json!({\n288\t            \"hits\": hits,\n289\t            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n290\t            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n291\t        });\n292\t        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n293\t        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n294\t            total_docs: 50,\n295\t            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n296\t            term_stats:      miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n317\t    ).await.unwrap();\n318\t\n319\t    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n320\t    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n321\t    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n322\t    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n323\t        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n324\t        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n325\t        offset: 5,\n326\t        limit: 5,\n327\t        filter: None,\n328\t        facets: None,\n329\t        ranking_score: true,\n330\t        body: json!({}),\n331\t        global_idf: None,\n332\t    };\n333\t    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n334\t        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n335\t        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n336\t    ).await.unwrap();\n337\t\n338\t    // Pages should not overlap\n339\t    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n340\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n341\t        .collect();\n342\t    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n343\t        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n344\t        .collect();\n345\t\n346\t    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n347\t    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n348\t\n349\t    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n350\t    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n351\t    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n352\t\n353\t    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n354\t    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n355\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n356\t        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n357\t    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n358\t        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n359\t        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n360\t    assert!(\n361\t        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n362\t        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n363\t        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n364\t    );\n365\t}\n366\t\n367\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n368\t// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n369\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n370\t\n371\t#[test]\n372\tfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n373\t    let shards = 4u32;\n374\t    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n375\t\n376\t    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n377\t    for i in 2..4u32 {\n378\t        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n379\t            .unwrap()\n380\t            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n381\t            .unwrap();\n382\t    }\n383\t\n384\t    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n385\t    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n386\t    for group in topo.groups() {\n387\t        if group.id == 1 {\n388\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n389\t            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t\n393\t    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n394\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n395\t        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n396\t        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n397\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n398\t                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n399\t            })\n400\t            .collect();\n401\t        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n402\t    }\n403\t}\n404\t\n405\t#[test]\n406\tfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n407\t    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n408\t    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n409\t    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n410\t    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n411\t    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n412\t\n413\t    let rf = 2usize;\n414\t    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n415\t    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n416\t\n417\t    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n418\t    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n419\t    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n420\t\n421\t    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n422\t    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n423\t    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n424\t\n425\t    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n426\t    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n427\t        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n428\t    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n429\t\n430\t    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n431\t    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n432\t    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n436\t// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n437\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n438\t\n439\t#[test]\n440\tfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n441\t    // Every MiroirCode MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n445\t        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n446\t        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n447\t\n448\t        // Must have all four fields\n449\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n450\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing code\", code);\n451\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing type\", code);\n452\t        assert!(parsed.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing link\", code);\n453\t\n454\t        // Code must start with miroir_\n455\t        let code_str = parsed[\"code\"].as_str().unwrap();\n456\t let meili_body = r#\"{\n483\t        \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n484\t        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n485\t        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n486\t        \"link\": Phase 2 DoD integration tests.\n//!\n//! Tests covering all Definition of Done criteria:\n//! - 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n//! - Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n//! - Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n//! - Offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n//! - Write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n//! - Error-format parity: every error matches Meilisearch shape\n//! - GET /_miroir/topology matches plan §10 shape\n\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{ErrorType, MeilisearchError, MiroirCode};\nuse miroir_core::merger::ScoreMergeStrategy;\nuse miroir_core::router::{shard_for_key, write_targets};\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{\n    MockNodeClient, SearchRequest,\n    dfs_query_then_fetch_search, plan_search_scatter,\n};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\nuse serde_json::{json, Value};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 3-node, 1 replica group topology\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn three_node_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 3);\n    for i in 0..3u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Helper: build a 2-group, 2-node-per-group topology (4 nodes)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfn two_group_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 2, 2);\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://localhost:810{i}\"),\n            1,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n// All MiroirCode variants for iteration\nconst ALL_CODES: [MiroirCode; 10] = [\n    MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n    MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n    MiroirCode::ShardUnavailable,\n    MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n    MiroirCode::IdempotencyKeyReused,\n    MiroirCode::SettingsVersionStale,\n    MiroirCode::MultiAliasNotWritable,\n    MiroirCode::JwtInvalid,\n    MiroirCode::JwtScopeDenied,\n    MiroirCode::InvalidAuth,\n];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 1: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_shard_assignment_coverage() {\n    let shards = 8u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut shard_counts: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let pk = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let shard = shard_for_key(&pk, shards);\n        shard_counts.entry(shard).or_default().push(pk);\n    }\n\n    // Every shard should have at least one document\n    assert_eq!(shard_counts.len(), shards as usize, \"all shards should receive documents\");\n\n    // All write targets should be reachable\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 3, \"RF=3 means 3 write targets per shard\");\n        for node_id in &targets {\n            assert!(topo.node(node_id).is_some(), \"target node {} should exist\", node_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Total count should be 1000\n    let total: usize = shard_counts.values().map(|v| v.len()).sum();\n    assert_eq!(total, 1000);\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 2: unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_deduplication() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Compute covering set for query_seq=0\n    let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, shards);\n\n    // Build per-node responses by accumulating all docs for each node.\n    // Multiple shards may map to the same node; a real Meilisearch node\n    // returns all its matching docs in a single response.\n    let mut node_hits: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<Value>> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut node_totals: HashMap<NodeId, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n        let doc1 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-a\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95,\n        });\n        let doc2 = json!({\n            \"id\": format!(\"doc-{}-b\", shard_id),\n            \"title\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", shard_id * 2 + 1),\n            \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.90,\n        });\n\n        let hits = node_hits.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default();\n        hits.push(doc1);\n        hits.push(doc2);\n        *node_totals.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 2;\n\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 100,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Now build one response per node with all its accumulated docs\n    for (node_id, hits) in node_hits {\n        let total = node_totals.remove(&node_id).unwrap();\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id, json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": total,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n        }));\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n    let req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test-index\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"unique-keyword\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 100,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: false,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    let result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &mock,\n        req,\n        &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .unwrap();\n\n    // Every document should appear exactly once (no duplicates)\n    let mut seen_ids: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    for hit in &result.hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap().to_string();\n        *seen_ids.entry(id).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    for (id, count) in &seen_ids {\n        assert_eq!(*count, 1, \"doc {} appeared {} times, expected 1\", id, count);\n    }\n\n    // Should find all 8 docs (2 per shard × 4 shards)\n    assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 8, \"expected 8 unique hits across 4 shards\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 3: facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    // Test the facet merge logic directly using the same algorithm the merger uses.\n    // Since merge_facets is private, we replicate the merge logic here to validate\n    // the aggregation contract.\n    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n    // Shard 0: red=10, green=5, blue=3\n    let mut shard0: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors0: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors0.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 10);\n    colors0.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 5);\n    colors0.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 3);\n    shard0.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors0);\n\n    // Shard 1: red=7, green=12, blue=8\n    let mut shard1: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    let mut colors1: BTreeMap<String, u64> = BTreeMap::new();\n    colors1.insert(\"red\".to_string(), 7);\n    colors1.insert(\"green\".to_string(), 12);\n    colors1.insert(\"blue\".to_string(), 8);\n    shard1.insert(\"color\".to_string(), colors1);\n\n    // Merge: sum per-value facet counts across shards\n    let mut merged: BTreeMap<String, BTreeMap<String, u64>> = BTreeMap::new();\n    for shard_facets in &[&shard0, &shard1] {\n        for (facet_name, values) in *shard_facets {\n            let entry = merged.entry(facet_name.clone()).or_default();\n            for (value, count) in values {\n                *entry.entry(value.clone()).or_insert(0) += count;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let colors = merged.get(\"color\").unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"red\").unwrap(), 17, \"red: 10+7=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"green\").unwrap(), 17, \"green: 5+12=17\");\n    assert_eq!(*colors.get(\"blue\").unwrap(), 11, \"blue: 3+8=11\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 4: offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let shards = 3u32;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shards);\n\n    // Each shard returns 5 hits with descending scores\n    let mut mock = MockNodeClient::default();\n\n    // Build covering set for page 1\n    let plan1 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 3, shards);\n\n    for (shard_id, node_id) in &plan1.shard_to_node {\n        let mut hits = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..5u32 {\n            hits.push(json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"s{}-d{}\", shard_id, i),\n                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n                \"_rankingScore\": 1.0 - (i as f64 * 0.1) - (*shard_id as f64 * 0.01),\n            }));\n        }\n        let response = json!({\n            \"hits\": hits,\n            \"estimatedTotalHits\": 5,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": 2,\n        });\n        mock.responses.insert(node_id.clone(), response);\n        mock.preflight_responses.insert(node_id.clone(), miroir_core::scatter::PreflightResponse {\n            total_docs: 50,\n            avg_doc_length: 500.0,\n            term_stats: HashMap::new(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Page 1: offset=0, limit=5\n    let req1 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 0,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result1 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan1, &mock, req1, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Page 2: offset=5, limit=5 (different query_seq to get different covering set)\n    let plan2 = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 1, 3, shards);\n    // Re-use same mock responses since the node set is the same for this topology\n    let req2 = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: \"test\".to_string(),\n        query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n        offset: 5,\n        limit: 5,\n        filter: None,\n        facets: None,\n        ranking_score: true,\n        body: json!({}),\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n    let result2 = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan2, &mock, req2, &topo,\n        miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &strategy,\n    ).await.unwrap();\n\n    // Pages should not overlap\n    let page1_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result1.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n    let page2_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = result2.hits.iter()\n        .filter_map(|h| h[\"id\"].as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n        .collect();\n\n    let overlap: std::collections::HashSet<_> = page1_ids.intersection(&page2_ids).collect();\n    assert!(overlap.is_empty(), \"pages should not overlap, but found: {:?}\", overlap);\n\n    // Combined should have 10 hits total (5 per page)\n    assert_eq!(result1.hits.len(), 5, \"page 1 should have 5 hits\");\n    assert_eq!(result2.hits.len(), 5, \"page 2 should have 5 hits\");\n\n    // Verify global ordering: page 1 scores >= page 2 scores\n    let page1_max_score = result1.hits.last()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(0.0);\n    let page2_min_score = result2.hits.first()\n        .and_then(|h| h[\"_rankingScore\"].as_f64())\n        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n    assert!(\n        page1_max_score >= page2_min_score,\n        \"page 1 min score ({}) should be >= page 2 max score ({})\",\n        page1_max_score, page2_min_score\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 5: write with one group completely down still succeeds + X-Miroir-Degraded\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_degraded_write_one_group_down() {\n    let shards = 4u32;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shards);\n\n    // Take down all nodes in group 1\n    for i in 2..4u32 {\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify group 1 nodes are not healthy\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        if group.id == 1 {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // For each shard, verify at least one group can still accept writes\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let targets = write_targets(shard_id, &topo);\n        let group0_targets: Vec<_> = targets.iter()\n            .filter(|node_id| {\n                topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.replica_group == 0).unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n        assert!(!group0_targets.is_empty(), \"shard {} should have group 0 targets\", shard_id);\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_quorum_logic_group_down() {\n    // Simulate: 2 groups, RF=2. Group 1 is completely down.\n    // Quorum per group = floor(2/2) + 1 = 2.\n    // Group 0: 2 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum met.\n    // Group 1: 0 of 2 nodes succeed → quorum not met.\n    // Overall: at least one group met quorum → write succeeds, degraded header.\n\n    let rf = 2usize;\n    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 2\n\n    // Group 0: 2 ACKs\n    let group0_acks = 2usize;\n    assert!(group0_acks >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 met quorum\");\n\n    // Group 1: 0 ACKs\n    let group1_acks = 0usize;\n    assert!(group1_acks < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 missed quorum\");\n\n    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_acks >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group met quorum\");\n\n    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group is degraded\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DoD 6: error-format parity with Meilisearch\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_shape_byte_for_byte_parity() {\n    // Every MiroirCode must produce a JSON object with exactly the keys:\n    // {message, code, type, link} where code starts with \"miroir_\".\n    for code in ALL_CODES {\n        let err = MeilisearchError::new(code, format!(\"test message for {:?}\", code));\n        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n\n        // Must have all four fields\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"message\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing message\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"code\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing code\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"type\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing type\", code);\n        assert!(parsed.get(\"link\").is_some(), \"{:?}: missing link\", code);\n\n        // Code must start with miroir_\n        let code_str = parsed[\"code\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            code_str.starts_with(\"miroir_\"),\n            \"{:?}: code '{}' should start with miroir_\",\n            code, code_str\n        );\n\n        // Type must be a valid Meilisearch error type\n        let type_str = parsed[\"type\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            [\"invalid_request\", \"auth\", \"internal\", \"system\"].contains(&type_str),\n            \"{:?}: type '{}' is not a valid Meilisearch error type\",\n            code, type_str\n        );\n\n        // Link must be a string pointing to docs\n        let link_str = parsed[\"link\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            link_str.contains(\"docs/errors.md\"),\n            \"{:?}: link '{}' should point to error docs\",\n            code, link_str\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_meilisearch_error_preserves_shape() {\n    let meili_body = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"Index `movies` not found.\",\n        \"code\": \"index_not_found\",\n        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n        \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#index_not_found\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let err = MeilisearchError::forwarded(meili_body).unwrap();\n    let roundtrip = serde_json::to_string(&err).unwrap();\n    let original: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(meili_body).unwrap();\n    let result: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&roundtrip).unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(original[\"message\"], result[\"message\"]);\n    assert_eq!(original[\"code\"], result[\"code\"]);\n    assert_eq!(original[\"type\"], result[\"type\"]);\n    assert_eq!(original[\"link\"], result[\"link\"]);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_document_not_found_error() {\n    let meili_body = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"Document `abc123` not found.\",\n        \"code\": \"document_not_found\",\n        \"type\": \"invalid_request\",\n        \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#document_not_found\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let err = MeilisearchError::forwarded(meili_body).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(err.code, \"document_not_found\");\n    assert_eq!(err.error_type, ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_forwarded_invalid_api_key_error() {\n    let meili_body = r#\"{\n        \"message\": \"The provided API key is invalid.\",\n        \"code\": \"invalid_api_key\",\n        \"type\": \"auth\",\n        \"link\": \"https://docs.meilisearch.com/errors#invalid_api_key\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let err = MeilisearchError::forwarded(meili_body).unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(err.code, \"invalid_api_key\");\n test_create_index_broadcasts_to_all_nodes() {\n68\t    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n69\t    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n70\t\n71\t    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n72\t        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n73\t        .with_status(200)\n74\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n75\t        .expect(1)\n76\t        .create_async()\n77\t        .await;\n78\t\n79\t    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n80\t        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n81\t        .with_status(200)\n82\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n83\t        .expect(1)\n84\t        .create_async()\n85\t        .await;\n86\t\n87\t    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n88\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n89\t    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n90\t\n91\t    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n92\t    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n93\t    let mut first_response: Option<serde_json::Value> = None;\n94\t    let mut all_ok = true;\n95\t\n96\t    for address in &nodes {\n97\t        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n98\t            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n99\t                if first_response.is_none() {\n100\t                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n101\t                }\n102\t                created_on.push(address.clone());\n103\t            }\n104\t            _ => {\n105\t                all_ok = false;\n106\t                break;\n107\t            }\n108\t        }\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    assert!(all_ok, \"all nodes should accept index creation\");\n112\t    assert_eq!(created_on.len(), 2);\n113\t\n114\t    mock1.assert_async().await;\n115\t    mock2.assert_async().await;\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Test: If the second node fails during index creation, the first node's index is rolled back.\n119\t#[tokio::test]\n120\tasync fn test_create_index_rollback_on_failure() {\n121\t    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n122\t    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n123\t\n124\t    // Node 1: create succeeds\n125\t    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n126\t        .with_status(200)\n127\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n128\t        .expect(1)\n129\t        .create_async()\n130\t        .await;\n131\t\n132\t    // Node 2: create fails\n133\t    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n134\t        .with_status(500)\n135\t        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"}).to_string())\n136\t        .expect(1)\n137\t        .create_async()\n138\t        .await;\n139\t\n140\t    // Rollback: delete on node 1\n141\t    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"DELETE\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n142\t        .with_status(200)\n143\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2}).to_string())\n144\t        .expect(1)\n145\t        .create_async()\n146\t        .await;\n147\t\n148\t    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n149\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n150\t    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n151\t\n152\t    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n153\t    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n154\t\n155\t    for address in &nodes {\n156\t        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n157\t            Ok((status, _)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n158\t                created_on.push(address.clone());\n159\t            }\n160\t            Ok((status, text)) => {\n161\t                // Rollback\n162\t                for addr in &created_on {\n163\t                    let _ = client.delete_raw(addr, \"/indexes/test-idx\").await;\n164\t                }\n165\t                assert!(created_on.len() == 1, \"first node should have been created before failure\");\n166\t                break;\n167\t            }\n168\t            Err(_) => break,\n169\t        }\n170\t    }\n171\t\n172\t    mock1.assert_async().await;\n173\t    mock2.assert_async().await;\n174\t    rollback1.assert_async().await;\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t// _miroir_shard in filterableAttributes\n179\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\t/// Test: After creating an index, _miroir_shard is in filterableAttributes.\n182\t#[tokio::test]\n183\tasync fn test_miroir_shard_in_filterable_attributes() {\n184\t    let mut server = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n185\t\n186\t    let mock = server.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n187\t        .with_status(200)\n188\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n189\t        .expect(1)\n190\t        .create_async()\n191\t        .await;\n192\t\n193\t    // GET settings returns current filterableAttributes\n194\t    let get_settings = server.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n195\t        .with_status(200)\n196\t        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"status\"], \"sortableAttributes\": []}).to_string())\n197\t        .expect(1)\n198\t        .create_async()\n199\t        .await;\n200\t\n201\t    // PATCH settings should include both \"status\" and \"_miroir_shard\"\n202\t    let patch_settings = server.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n203\t        .match_body(mockito::Matcher::JsonString(json!({\n204\t            \"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\", \"status\"]\n205\t        }).to_string()))\n206\t        .with_status(200)\n207\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2}).to_string())\n208\t        .expect(1)\n209\t        .create_async()\n210\t        .await;\n211\t\n212\t    let config = make_config(vec![server.url()]);\n213\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n214\t    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n215\t\n216\t    // Step 1: Create index\n217\t    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n218\t    let (status, _) = client.post_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes\", &body).await.unwrap();\n219\t    assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n220\t\n221\t    // Step 2: Read current settings and merge _miroir_shard\n222\t    let mut merged_attrs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = vec![json!(\"_miroir_shard\")];\n223\t    if let Ok((s, text)) = client.get_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\").await {\n224\t        if s >= 200 && s < 300 {\n225\t            if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&text) {\n226\t                if let Some(existing) = settings.get(\"filterableAttributes\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n227\t                    for attr in existing {\n228\t                        let attr_str = attr.as_str().unwrap_or(\"\");\n229\t                        if attr_str != \"_miroir_shard\" && !attr_str.is_empty() {\n230\t                            merged_attrs.push(attr.clone());\n231\t                        }\n232\t                    }\n233\t                }\n234\t            }\n235\t        }\n236\t    }\n237\t\n238\t    // Step 3: PATCH with merged filterableAttributes\n239\t    let patch = json!({\"filterableAttributes\": merged_attrs});\n240\t    let (status, _) = client.patch_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\", &patch).await.unwrap();\n241\t    assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n242\t\n243\t    mock.assert_async().await;\n244\t    get_settings.assert_async().await;\n245\t    patch_settings.assert_async().await;\n246\t}\n247\t\n248\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n249\t// Stats aggregation — logical document count\n250\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n251\t\n252\t/// Test: numberOfDocuments is divided by RG*RF to get logical count.\n253\t#[test]\n254\tfn test_stats_logical_doc_count() {\n255\t    let rg = 2u64;\n256\t    let rf = 1u64;\n257\t    let divisor = rg * rf;\n258\t\n259\t    // Simulate: 3 nodes each reporting 100 docs\n260\t    // RG=2, RF=1: nodes 0,1 in group 0, node 2 in group 1\n261\t    // Total raw = 300, logical = 300 / 2 = 150\n262\t    let total_docs: u64 = 300;\n263\t    let logical = total_docs / divisor;\n264\t    assert_eq!(logical, 150);\n265\t\n266\t    // Simulate: 2 nodes, RG=1, RF=1: both in same group\n267\t    // Total raw = 200, divisor = 1, logical = 200\n268\t    let rg1 = 1u64;\n269\t    let rf1 = 1u64;\n270\t    let logical_rg1 = 200u64 / (rg1 * rf1);\n271\t    assert_eq!(logical_rg1, 200);\n272\t}\n273\t\n274\t/// Test: fieldDistribution is summed per-field across nodes.\n275\t#[test]\n276\tfn test_field_distribution_merge() {\n277\t    use std::collections::HashMap;\n278\t\n279\t    let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n280\t\n281\t    // Node 1\n282\t    let fd1 = json!({\"title\": 100, \"body\": 200});\n283\t    if let Some(obj) = fd1.as_object() {\n284\t        for (field, count) in obj {\n285\t            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n286\t                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n287\t            }\n288\t        }\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    // Node 2\n292\t    let fd2 = json!({\"title\": 150, \"body\": 250, \"tags\": 50});\n293\t    if let Some(obj) = fd2.as_object() {\n294\t        for (field, count) in obj {\n295\t            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n296\t                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n297\t            }\n298\t        }\n299\t    }\n300\t\n301\t    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"title\").unwrap_or(&0), 250);\n302\t    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"body\").unwrap_or(&0), 450);\n303\t    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"tags\").unwrap_or(&0), 50);\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n307\t// Settings sequential broadcast with rollback\n308\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n309\t\n310\t/// Test: Settings update fails on node 2, triggering rollback on node 1.\n311\t#[tokio::test]\n312\tasync fn test_settings_broadcast_rollback() {\n313\t    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n314\t    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n315\t\n316\t    // Snapshot current settings from node 1\n317\t    let get1 = server1.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n318\t        .with_status(200)\n319\t        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]}).to_string())\n320\t        .expect(1)\n321\t        .create_async()\n322\t        .await;\n323\t\n324\t    // Snapshot from node 2\n325\t    let get2 = server2.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n326\t        .with_status(200)\n327\t        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]}).to_string())\n328\t        .expect(1)\n329\t        .create_async()\n330\t        .await;\n331\t\n332\t    // PATCH succeeds on node 1\n333\t    let patch1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n334\t        .with_status(200)\n335\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 10}).to_string())\n336\t        .expect(1)\n337\t        .create_async()\n338\t        .await;\n339\t\n340\t    // PATCH fails on node 2\n341\t    let patch2_fail = server2.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n342\t        .with_status(500)\n343\t        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"}).to_string())\n344\t        .expect(1)\n345\t        .create_async()\n346\t        .await;\n347\t\n348\t    // Rollback: restore original settings on node 1\n349\t    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n350\t        .match_body(mockito::Matcher::JsonString(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]}).to_string()))\n351\t        .with_status(200)\n352\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 11}).to_string())\n353\t        .expect(1)\n354\t        .create_async()\n355\t        .await;\n356\t\n357\t    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n358\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n359\t    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n360\t\n361\t    let settings_path = \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\";\n362\t    let new_settings = json!({\"rankingRules\": [\"typo\", \"words\"]});\n363\t\n364\t    // Snapshot phase\n365\t    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)> = Vec::new();\n366\t    for address in &nodes {\n367\t        let (status, text) = client.get_raw(address, settings_path).await.unwrap();\n368\t        assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n369\t        snapshots.push((address.clone(), serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap()));\n370\t    }\n371\t\n372\t    // Apply sequentially - node 1 succeeds, node 2 fails\n373\t    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n374\t    for (address, _) in &snapshots {\n375\t        match client.patch_raw(address, settings_path, &new_settings).await {\n376\t            Ok((status, _)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n377\t                applied.push(address.clone());\n378\t            }\n379\t            _ => {\n380\t                // Rollback\n381\t                for addr in &applied {\n382\t                    if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == addr) {\n383\t                        let _ = client.patch_raw(addr, settings_path, snapshot).await;\n384\t                    }\n385\t                }\n386\t                break;\n387\t            }\n388\t        }\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    get1.assert_async().await;\n392\t    get2.assert_async().await;\n393\t    patch1.assert_async().await;\n394\t    patch2_fail.assert_async().await;\n395\t    rollback1.assert_async().await;\n396\t}\n397\t\n398\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n399\t// DELETE /indexes/{uid} — broadcast\n400\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n401\t\n402\t/// Test: Deleting an index sends DELETE to every node.\n403\t#[tokio::test]\n404\tasync fn test_delete_index_broadcasts_to_all_nodes() {\n405\t    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n406\t    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n407\t\n408\t    let mock1 .create_async()\n413\t        .await;\n414\t\n415\t    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"DELETE\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n416\t        .with_status(200)\n417\t        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n418\t        .expect(1)\n419\t        .create_async()\n420\t        .await;\n421\t\n422\t    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n423\t    let client =   for address in &nodes {\n428\t        let (status, _) = client.delete_raw(address, \"/indexes/test-idx\").await.unwrap();\n429\t        if status >= 200 && status < 300 {\n430\t            success_count += 1;\n431\t        }\n432\t    }\n433\t\n434\t    assert_eq!(success_count, 2);\n435\t    mock1.assert_async().await;\n436\t    mock2.assert_async().await;\n437\t}\n438\t\n439\t// .create_async()\n494\t        .await;\n495\t\n496\t    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/keys\")\n497\t        .with_status(500)\n498\t        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal .await;\n510\t\n511\t    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n512\t    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n513\t    let nodes: Vec<String> = ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n539\t// PATCH /indexes/{uid} — update index metadata with rollback\n540\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n541\t\n542\t/// Test: Index metadata update with snapshot and rollback.\n543\t#[tokio::test]\n544\tasync fn test_update_index_snapshot_and_rollback() {\n545\t    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n546\t    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n547\t\n548\t    // Snapshot from both nodes\n549\t    let get1 = server1.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n550\t        .with_status(200)\n551\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"primaryKey\": \"id\"}).to_string())\n552\t        .expect(1)\n553\t        .create_async()\n554\t        .await;\n555\t\n556\t    let get2 = server2.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n557\t        .with_status(200)\n558\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"primaryKey\": \"id\"}).to_string())\n559\t        .expect(1)\n560\t        .create_async()\n561\t        .await;\n562\t\n563\t    // PATCH succeeds on node 1\n564\t    let patch1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n565\t        .with_status(200)\n566\t        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": server2.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n573\t        .with_status(500)\n574\t        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"error\"}).to_string())\n575\t        .expect(1)\n576\t        .create_async()\n577\t        .await;\n578\t\n579\t    // Rollback on node 1\n580\t    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n581\t      })\n        .collect();\n\n    MiroirConfig {\n        master_key: \"test-master-key\".into(),\n        node_master_key: \"test-node-master-key\".into(),\n        shards: 64,\n ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: Creating an index sends POST /indexes to every configured node.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_create_index_broadcasts_to_all_nodes() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", .match_header(\"Authorization\", \"Bearer test-node-master-key\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1, \"status\": \"enqueued\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, text)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                if first_response.is_none() {\n                    first_response = serde_json::from_str(&text).ok();\n                }\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            _ => {\n                all_ok = false;\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert!(all_ok, \"all nodes should accept index creation\");\n    assert_eq!(created_on.len(), 2);\n\n    mock1.assert_async().await;\n    mock2.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n/// Test: If the second node fails during index creation, the first node's index is rolled back.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_create_index_rollback_on_failure() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Node 1: create succeeds\n    let mock1 = server1.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Node 2: create fails\n    let mock2 = server2.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .with_status(500)\n        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Rollback: delete on node 1\n    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"DELETE\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n    let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n    for address in &nodes {\n        match client.post_raw(address, \"/indexes\", &body).await {\n            Ok((status, _)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            Ok((status, text)) => {\n                // Rollback\n                for addr in &created_on {\n                    let _ = client.delete_raw(addr, \"/indexes/test-idx\").await;\n                }\n                assert!(created_on.len() == 1, \"first node should have been created before failure\");\n                break;\n            }\n            Err(_) => break,\n        }\n    }\n\n    mock1.assert_async().await;\n    mock2.assert_async().await;\n    rollback1.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// _miroir_shard in filterableAttributes\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: After creating an index, _miroir_shard is in filterableAttributes.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_miroir_shard_in_filterable_attributes() {\n    let mut server = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    let mock = server.mock(\"POST\", \"/indexes\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"taskUid\": 1}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // GET settings returns current filterableAttributes\n    let get_settings = server.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"status\"], \"sortableAttributes\": []}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // PATCH settings should include both \"status\" and \"_miroir_shard\"\n    let patch_settings = server.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .match_body(mockito::Matcher::JsonString(json!({\n            \"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\", \"status\"]\n        }).to_string()))\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": 2}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    // Step 1: Create index\n    let body = json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"});\n    let (status, _) = client.post_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes\", &body).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n\n    // Step 2: Read current settings and merge _miroir_shard\n    let mut merged_attrs: Vec<serde_json::Value> = vec![json!(\"_miroir_shard\")];\n    if let Ok((s, text)) = client.get_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\").await {\n        if s >= 200 && s < 300 {\n            if let Ok(settings) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&text) {\n                if let Some(existing) = settings.get(\"filterableAttributes\").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {\n                    for attr in existing {\n                        let attr_str = attr.as_str().unwrap_or(\"\");\n                        if attr_str != \"_miroir_shard\" && !attr_str.is_empty() {\n                            merged_attrs.push(attr.clone());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Step 3: PATCH with merged filterableAttributes\n    let patch = json!({\"filterableAttributes\": merged_attrs});\n    let (status, _) = client.patch_raw(&nodes[0], \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\", &patch).await.unwrap();\n    assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n\n    mock.assert_async().await;\n    get_settings.assert_async().await;\n    patch_settings.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Stats aggregation — logical document count\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: numberOfDocuments is divided by RG*RF to get logical count.\n#[test]\nfn test_stats_logical_doc_count() {\n    let rg = 2u64;\n    let rf = 1u64;\n    let divisor = rg * rf;\n\n    // Simulate: 3 nodes each reporting 100 docs\n    // RG=2, RF=1: nodes 0,1 in group 0, node 2 in group 1\n    // Total raw = 300, logical = 300 / 2 = 150\n    let total_docs: u64 = 300;\n    let logical = total_docs / divisor;\n    assert_eq!(logical, 150);\n\n    // Simulate: 2 nodes, RG=1, RF=1: both in same group\n    // Total raw = 200, divisor = 1, logical = 200\n    let rg1 = 1u64;\n    let rf1 = 1u64;\n    let logical_rg1 = 200u64 / (rg1 * rf1);\n    assert_eq!(logical_rg1, 200);\n}\n\n/// Test: fieldDistribution is summed per-field across nodes.\n#[test]\nfn test_field_distribution_merge() {\n    use std::collections::HashMap;\n\n    let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Node 1\n    let fd1 = json!({\"title\": 100, \"body\": 200});\n    if let Some(obj) = fd1.as_object() {\n        for (field, count) in obj {\n            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Node 2\n    let fd2 = json!({\"title\": 150, \"body\": 250, \"tags\": 50});\n    if let Some(obj) = fd2.as_object() {\n        for (field, count) in obj {\n            if let Some(c) = count.as_u64() {\n                *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += c;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"title\").unwrap_or(&0), 250);\n    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"body\").unwrap_or(&0), 450);\n    assert_eq!(*field_distribution.get(\"tags\").unwrap_or(&0), 50);\n}\n\n// .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], \"rankingRules\": [\"words\"]}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // PATCH succeeds on node 1\n    let patch1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"taskUid\": .with_status(500)\n        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Rollback: restore original settings on node 1\n    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx/settings\")\n        .match_body(mockito::Matcher::JsonString(json!({\"filterableAttributes\": [\"_miroir_shard\"], 200 && status < 300);\n        snapshots.push((address.clone(), serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap()));\n    }\n\n    // Apply sequentially - node 1 succeeds, node 2 fails\n    let mut applied:    break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    get1.assert_async().await;\n    get2.assert_async().await;\n    patch1.assert_async().await;\n    patch2_fail.assert_async().await;\n    rollback1.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// DELETE /indexes/{uid} — broadcast\n// /indexes/{uid} — update index metadata with rollback\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: Index metadata update with snapshot and rollback.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_update_index_snapshot_and_rollback() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Snapshot from both nodes\n    let get1 = server1.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"primaryKey\": \"id\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let get2 = server2.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"primaryKey\": \"id\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // PATCH succeeds on node 1\n    let patch1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"primaryKey\": \"new_id\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // PATCH fails on node 2\n    let patch2 = server2.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .with_status(500)\n        .with_body(json!({\"message\": \"error\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    // Rollback on node 1\n    let rollback1 = server1.mock(\"PATCH\", \"/indexes/test-idx\")\n        .match_body(mockito::Matcher::JsonString(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\", \"primaryKey\": \"id\"}).to_string()))\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"uid\": \"test-idx\"}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    let update_body = json!({\"primaryKey\": \"new_id\"});\n\n    // Snapshot phase\n    let mut snapshots: Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)> = Vec::new();\n    for address in &nodes {\n        let (status, text) = client.get_raw(address, \"/indexes/test-idx\").await.unwrap();\n        assert!(status >= 200 && status < 300);\n        snapshots.push((address.clone(), serde_json::from_str(&text).unwrap()));\n    }\n\n    // Apply sequentially\n    let mut applied: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    for (address, _) in &snapshots {\n        match client.patch_raw(address, \"/indexes/test-idx\", &update_body).await {\n            Ok((status, _)) if status >= 200 && status < 300 => {\n                applied.push(address.clone());\n            }\n            _ => {\n                for addr in &applied {\n                    if let Some((_, snapshot)) = snapshots.iter().find(|(a, _)| a == addr) {\n                        let _ = client.patch_raw(addr, \"/indexes/test-idx\", snapshot).await;\n                    }\n                }\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    get1.assert_async().await;\n    get2.assert_async().await;\n    patch1.assert_async().await;\n    patch2.assert_async().await;\n    rollback1.assert_async().await;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Stats fan-out with RG=2\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// Test: Stats aggregation divides by RG*RF for logical doc count.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_stats_fan_out_logical_count() {\n    let mut server1 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n    let mut server2 = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n    // Each node reports 100 docs\n    let stats1 = server1.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/stats\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 100, \"isIndexing\": false, \"fieldDistribution\": {\"title\": 100}}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let stats2 = server2.mock(\"GET\", \"/indexes/test-idx/stats\")\n        .with_status(200)\n        .with_body(json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 100, \"isIndexing\": false, \"fieldDistribution\": {\"title\": 100, \"body\": 50}}).to_string())\n        .expect(1)\n        .create_async()\n        .await;\n\n    let config = make_config_rg2(vec![server1.url(), server2.url()]);\n    let client = MeilisearchClient::new(config.node_master_key.clone());\n    let nodes: Vec<String> = config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect();\n\n    let mut total_docs: u64 = 0;\n    for address in &nodes {\n        let result = client.get_index_stats(address, \"test-idx\").await;\n        if let Ok(stats) = result {\n            if let Some(n) = stats.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                total_docs += n;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // RG=2, RF=1 → divisor = 2\n    let rg = config.replica_groups as u64;\n    let rf = config.replication_factor as u64;\n    let logical = total_docs / (rg * rf);\n\n    assert_eq!(logical, 100, \"logical doc count should be total/2 for RG=2 RF=1\");\n\n    stats1.assert_async().await;\n    stats2.assert_async().await;\n}\n","numLines":676,"startLine":1,"totalLines":676}}}
